category_title: Fourth-grade students who initially dislike reading but engage through self-selection
minimal_context: The student is participating in an individual reading conference at a side table to discuss their self-selected book.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's have our individual reading conference. What do you notice about this book?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't know, I'm just reading it.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Can you tell me about the characters?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: There's a boy and a dog. I read the whole first half already.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What are your thoughts about the reading to put on the website?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't really have any thoughts about it. I'm just reading it cover to cover so I can finish.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's have our individual reading conference. What do you notice about this book?

STUDENT: I don't know, I'm just reading it.

TUTOR: Can you tell me about the characters?

STUDENT: There's a boy and a dog. I read the whole first half already.

TUTOR: What are your thoughts about the reading to put on the website?

STUDENT: I don't really have any thoughts about it. I'm just reading it cover to cover so I can finish.

task.content: Participating in individual reading conferences and answering questions about book features and characters
task.learning_goal: Make them really think deeply about their book

context.environment: Table to the side of the classroom
context.materials: Self-selected books, written response letters, password-protected website

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When reading books independently without prompts
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Simply reading it cover to cover
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Not thinking deeply about their book

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Simply reading it cover to cover
